response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished software craftsman with strong full-stack and D/TypeScript/Node.js experience, but the role requires 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or distributed compute platforms. Their C++ exposure is limited to ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and there is no evidence of cloud compute platform leadership, distributed systems at scale, or formal technical leadership of a platform team. The profile is a poor match for a C++-centric Cloud Compute Platform Tech Lead role.
strengths: Long, varied senior-level engineering career with mentoring, workshops, and teaching
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing culture
Some distributed/high-throughput experience (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of req/s)
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum — only a brief NI C/C++ stint a decade ago
No demonstrated cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure leadership
No formal tech-lead-of-platform-team experience mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / distributed infra leadership
Strong engineering craftsmanship and mentoring track record
